title: [InstCombine] Read-only call without return can capture
summary:
The generalization broadened the optimization from a specific memcpy-to-alloca pattern to handle any read-only, non-capturing call that uses an alloca initialized from constant memory. The key insight was recognizing that the original pattern's safety condition—ensuring the alloca isn't modified after initialization—applies whenever a call doesn't capture the pointer and only reads memory.

The patch simplifies the safety check from two complex conditions to a single unified rule: if a call doesn't capture the alloca pointer and only reads memory (either globally or for that specific argument), it can be ignored when determining if the alloca is only copied from constant memory. This enables the transformation to work across:
- Different address spaces (via addrspacecast)
- Various memcpy variants
- Both nocapture and may-capture calls (provided they're read-only)
- Multiple subsequent read-only operations

The approach demonstrates how to generalize by identifying the core safety invariants (no capture + read-only) rather than pattern-matching specific instruction sequences, making the optimization more robust and applicable to future LLVM IR variations.
